fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Illinois Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer occupational therapist license to illinois, occupational therapist compact illinois, illinois occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Illinois IDFPR — Occupational Therapy
board.parent_dept: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)
board.mailing_address: IDFPR, 320 W. Washington St., Springfield, IL 62786

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Illinois.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Review Licensure Requirements
steps.description: Visit the IDFPR Occupational Therapist webpage and carefully review the 'Application for Licensure as an Occupational Therapist' form and the detailed requirements for licensure, including education, fieldwork, and examination.

steps.title: Create IDFPR Online Services Portal Account
steps.description: Navigate to the IDFPR Online Services Portal, register for a new account by providing your personal information, and then log in to access the application system.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Within the IDFPR Online Services Portal, locate and complete the online 'Application for Licensure as an Occupational Therapist,' ensuring all necessary personal and professional details are accurately provided.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited occupational therapy program, verification of supervised fieldwork (typically Part III of the application form completed by your supervisor), and your official NBCOT score report to be sent directly to IDFPR.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required $90 application fee securely through the IDFPR Online Services Portal using a credit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application via the IDFPR Online Services Portal and respond promptly to any requests from IDFPR for additional information or documentation.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Illinois OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Illinois have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Illinois
hero.dek: Occupational Therapist license applications for Illinois are processed through the IDFPR Online Services Portal, managed by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR).

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the IDFPR Online Services Portal, click 'Register' for new users, and follow the prompts to create an account by providing personal details, a username, and password.
